title: andryala pinnatifida subsp. preauxiana
intro: Andryala pinnatifida subsp. preauxiana is a subspecies of Andryala pinnatifida, a plant species in the family Asteraceae, characterized by its fern-like foliage and small, yellow flowers. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Andryala pinnatifida subsp. preauxiana include alleviating symptoms of colds and flu, soothing digestive problems, and reducing inflammation in the body. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
